goal: Tune the live openclaw-benben vNext /lite local lane boundaries so local status is inspectable, adult creative prompts are passed to the local adapter, and clear piracy or paid-bypass requests fast-refuse before the local model can hang.
unchanged_live_items: Telegram and QQBot entries on openclaw-benben

synthetic_live_contract.mode: in-process fake adapter on NAS using the live plugin file
checks: /lite status returns model/provider/timeout/ctx without calling adapter
/lite status redacts URL credentials and query values
piracy prompt fast-refuses and does not call adapter
adminAI service marker does not activate the plugin

behavior_contract: /lite status is a local-lane control/status command and should not invoke the model adapter.
Adult creative prompts are no longer blocked by a coarse plugin-level keyword gate.
Clear piracy, paid-bypass, and infringement-download instructions fast-refuse before local model invocation.
The local adapter request remains sanitized and carries the session key only as sha256.
The default local adapter timeout is 45 seconds unless localLane.timeoutMs overrides it.
Slash commands, cloud switches, and model switches still fall through to OpenClaw/vNext command handling.
rollback: Verify 18792 /health, Feishu websocket ready, and synthetic /lite contract.
residual_risks: This was validated with a synthetic in-process fake adapter, not by sending a new real Feishu user message after restart.
Keyword-based fast refusal may still need tuning for edge cases where the user asks about piracy in a purely educational or anti-piracy context.
The local model's own output behavior is still model-dependent once a prompt reaches the adapter.
